description.content: Generate and manage user tokens for authenticating API calls. The authorize mutation generates a user token from a master token.
Access the GraphQL Playground interface for exploring the Tray Embedded API interactively. This is only available for development and testing purposes.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Generates a user access token from a master token for a specific user. The returned token is used as a Bearer token for user-scoped API calls such as managing solution instances and authentications. Requires a master token.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Authentication failed. The bearer token is missing, invalid, or does not have sufficient permissions for the requested operation.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Create, retrieve, and delete third-party service authentications that power Tray connectors (e.g., Salesforce, Slack).

Creates a new authentication for a third-party service (e.g., Salesforce, Slack) for a specific user. Returns an authId that can be used when creating or updating solution instances. Note that OAuth-based authentications cannot be created via API unless you own the service. Requires a user token.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Authentication failed. The bearer token is missing, invalid, or does not have sufficient permissions for the requested operation.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Call any Tray connector operation to pull data from a particular service and display it in your application.

Calls any Tray connector operation to pull data from a particular third-party service. Used in combination with a user token and authentication ID to interact with service connector operations (e.g., list Salesforce objects, get Slack channels). Returns a promise that resolves to the output of the connector call. Requires a user token.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Authentication failed. The bearer token is missing, invalid, or does not have sufficient permissions for the requested operation.

Create, update, enable, disable, and delete solution instances for end users. Requires a user token for most operations.
Creates a new solution instance from a published solution for a specific end user. Accepts the solutionId, instanceName, and optional authValues and configValues. Requires a user token.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

The Tray.ai Embedded API is a GraphQL-based API that allows partners and customers to present in-app embedded integration experiences using Tray's UI components. It provides programmatic access to manage users, solutions, solution instances, authentications, workflows, and connector operations. All API calls are made via HTTP POST to the GraphQL endpoint with Bearer token authentication. The API supports two token types: master tokens (for admin operations like managing users) and user tokens (for user-scoped operations like managing solution instances). This is a backend-only API; client-side JavaScript calls are blocked by CORS.
